properties.name: PCT Section A (Campo to Warner Springs)
PCT Section B (Warner Springs to I-10)
PCT Section C (I-10 to current camp)
Day 1 - Camp
properties.description:
Bag weight = 36.2  lbs.
(With 5 Liters of water and 3 days of food)
Goal to hike ~ 1,100 miles 
(or halfway point of 1,325 miles 🤪)

Day #1 : Happy Easter 🐰🐣  04/05/2026 - Sunday
Miles Hiked today = 4.94
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 4.94
Miles to go = 1,095.06 // 1320.06

Drive with Devon & Paul & Sheldon
Later start than planned... On road by 10 AM 
~ 5 hour drive to to trailhead

Devon packed road snacks and breakfast.
Egg and cheese burritos and pastries.
Rest stop for pee break 
Late lunch at the Burrito Factory in El Centro
Ate in the car - what a mess! 

Made it to Campo and the PCT trailhead 
Tooka few pictures and started on trail @ 3:40 PM
Beautiful chunky rocks amidst trees and shrubs - dappled shade and rolling trail
A few road crossings (dirt and paved) and a railroad crossing.
Wooden bridge over a creek - met a few hikers
Set-up camp early just shy of five miles.
Snacks for dinner (not super hungry after car burritos).

Camping here: GPS:[32.60923, -116.50450] 

Learning curve on tent is large, still a little nervous about setting it up under certain conditions - but many miles to figure things out. 
Sandy ground makes getting guide lines taught and tent stakes secure a challenge - 🤞 finger crossed it holds for the night.

Sun set and the animals start making noise. So many birds chirping and owls hooting. The coyotes are noisy tonight too! 

Charging up phone and reading until I fall asleep - early night.

Did NOT sleep good - tooo loud - chirping frog (?!) every 10 seconds for 2-3 minutes, coyote screams, barking dogs...

properties.name: Day 2 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #2:  04/06/2026 - Monday
Goal: Hiking to malts and burgers at Lake Moreno
Miles Hiked Today = 18.18 miles
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 22.4
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 23.12
Miles to go = 1,077.6 miles // 1302.6

Poor sleep - up early before 5 AM
Bathroom break and reading in the tent until 5:30 AM
Packup and hike a few miles before breakfast.
Rolling rocky hills - found a HUGE boulder for breakfast of tofu scramble.

Overcast and cloudy all morning - crisp and beautiful.
Up a bit with rolling hills covered in boulders and wild flowers along the trail.
Met a few people on trail.

Stopped for lunch and a bit of rain came down while we ate lunch. Rain stopped as we started hiking.
Hit a road walk and trail magic of a big water cache at mile 10. Filled up just as a ranger pulled up for a PCT permit check and CA fire permit check.

Sun has come out in full force - getting hot.
Road walk and connect back to trail down into ravine before a large incline up up up to get over the mountain. Stopped half way up to drench shirts in water for a cool down and more up up up.

Lake Morena visible in the distance.
More up followed by a nice steady down into Moreno Village.

Trail magic at trailhead - hikers from the prior year came back to give back to the trail - cooler of cold beverages - I had a Watermelon High Noon. 🍉 

Walked into town to the Malt shop - many hikers assembled on the patio. Ate a bacon mushroom Swiss burger, fries, Arnold Palmer & salted caramel malt with whip cream. So full!

Walked back to trail and got water before one last 2 mile push to setup camp.  Ground is Bouldery and finding a spot was difficult to setup - on a bit of a slant 🫤

Camping here: GPS: [32.69673, -116.49535] 

Very tired - will read in tent and likely fall asleep early.
Slept so good last night - so very quiet. 😴

properties.name: Day 3 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #3: 04/07/2026 - Tuesday 
Goal: Early start & avoid heat
Miles Hiked Today = 14.76
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 37.2
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 37.88
Miles to go = 1,062.8 miles // 1287.80

Up at 5:30 AM - packed and on trail.
Sky still had color - pinks and purples.
Last glimpse of Lake Moreno as we head on. 
Views of mountains and big chunky boulders.

First cow sighting - a whole herd right near our expected water source. Stopped for breakfast and checked maps - thankfully better water about 1/2 mile ahead. 

Walked to water - spigot at campground. 
Loaded up for the day (for the ups ahead).
Crossed under Hwy 88 and saw it in the distance all morning as we hiked.

Caterpillar hitchhikers all day. 🐛
They are out in full force and impossible to avoid.
I found several throughout the day catching a ride on my shirt, backpack, hat, pants... Curious what kind of creature they will turn into...

Rocks are turning from rolly polly boulders to spires and pokey chunks - still many wildflowers all around and near the trail.

Did a shirt soak to cool down in the wide open sun. Trail AC.

Hot and sunny today - zero cloud cover.
Skipped the swimming hole and opted to hike ahead for a shady lunch spot and some reading followed by a nap...

Took off shoes when relaxing and discovered my first blisters on the inside of my big toe (3 in a row) & a hotspot on the other big toe in the same spot 😔
(Fun fact: I didn't get a single blister when hiking the AZT last year!) Cleaned and elevated feet while resting.

Decided to push ahead in the heat and sun for another 5 miles up up up to camping and next water source.

Passed through an "unexploded military ordinance" site - WTF?! Success - we didn't explode!🤪

Devon struggled with the heat - made shade stops and slowed down. Made it to camp and a beautiful cold clear water stream.

Setup tents, filtered water, and had dinner with other hikers. We are camping with Maddie, & Tina.

Talked about books and podcasts and food (always food.) Temperature is dropping - everyone turned in early to stay warm and rest.

Camping here: GPS:[32.81354, -116.41853]

properties.name: Day 4 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #4: 04/08/2026 - Wednesday 
Goal: Breakfast & 1st mail pickup @ Mt. Laguna
Miles Hiked Today = 10.48
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 47.5
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 48.36
Miles to go = 1,052.5 miles // 1277.50

Slept in and on trail by 7 AM.
Short rolling uphill hike - Mexico visible in the distance. 
Terrain changed drastically to beautiful pine forest with giant pine cones (as big as my hand).

Pine House Cafe for breakfast with a bunch of hikers.
Sat on the patio - Cottonwood tree seeding - puffs floating everywhere - feels like it's snowing. Beautiful - but also annoying and impossible to keep out of food.

Arnold Palmer, water, side salad, fries and french baguette ham sandwich. Crepe for dessert.

Hung out until noon (waiting for the post office to open) and walked to pickup first resupply box. Sooo much food - likely more than needed.

Repacked bags and headed out of town. 

Vista when getting on trail is breathtaking and mind boggling!! Desert mountains as far as the eye can see!

Terrain changing again, fewer palm trees more shrubs. Trail twists and turns with views every so often of the desert mountains. Telescope and towers visible.

Big fat Snake on trail (NOT a rattlesnake) - looks like he just had dinner!🐍 

Hiked down and up a valley to another lookout. Hitched a ride to Laguna campground (due to restrictions for camping in next several miles).

First hitchhike on the PCT - rode in the truck bed with our bags and with Paul & Sky.

Hung out with sky at the campground. Made dinner - spicy pasta (made me sniffle and tear up a bit) - delicious!

Feel very fancy having a table to cook/eat at and running water and bathrooms (even if they're just put toilets.

Another blister on my right foot this time 🤬 
Not sure what's going on with my feet but they aren't happy - hopefully all these blisters hurry up and turn into calluses real quick!

Camping here: GPS:[32.88939, -116.44854]

properties.name: Day 5 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #5: 04/09/2026 - Thursday 
Goal: 15+ miles (halfway to Julian)
Miles Hiked Today = 16.75 miles
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 63.7
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 65.11
Miles to go = 1,036.3 miles // 1261.30

Up at 5:30 AM and packing up
Bougie breakfast of peach cobbler MRE & coffee (first cup since getting on trail)

Road walked back to trail head (~1/2 mile)
Greeted with beautiful desert mountain views

Overcast and blustery in the AM

Hit the 50 mile marker on trail 🥳 

Hiked through rolling hills green with brush and shrubs. Still suffering from hitchhiker caterpillars - they are everywhere and cover most bushes you walk past - can't avoid them catching a ride.

Stopped after 5 miles at picnic site for snacks and a break - shoes off and feet up laying on the picnic table = slice of heaven. Filled up on water and hiked on.

Trail climbs to what looks like an old road with crazy scary overlook of desert mountains below. 

Memorial on rock face along trail & overlook.

Hiked on some sketchy ledges up and around bends on trail with scary drop offs...

More rolling green hills. Some have big rolly polly boulders & boulder fields.

Rattlesnake on edge of trail - rattled us and slid away.

Jackrabbit sighting in the shrubs - he was so scared of us!

Saw a Bird flying with snake in mouth.

Water party at big spigot on trail - met and chatted with some other hikers.

Hiked a few more miles down down down into a valley and setup tents.

Chatted with another hiker ____ over dinner (peanut butter pasta was delicious, especially with extra peanut butter added in!).

Finished setting up camp and into the tent.

Camping here: GPS:[33.06108, -116.57739]

properties.name: Day 6 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #6: 04/10/2027 - Friday 
Goal: Unknown - possibly head into town of Julian ?! 
Miles Hiked Today = 15.96 miles
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 79.7 
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 81.07
Miles to go = 1,020.3 miles // 1245.30

Morning pack up and on trail by 6:30 AM
Start with a road climb up up up 

Cruisey day afterwards - beautiful views of green mountains - can look behind and see where we came from last night.

Trail follows the edge of mountains for a few miles. Some spots are a little sketchy - but safe of you go slow.

Found a nice shady spot for peanut butter snack break and shoes off. 

Landscape changing to big boulder mountains and hills. 

Eventually made our way down and had a long straight walk to get to the scissors road crossing and under the bridge.

Handful of hikers hanging out to wait out the heat before the climb.

We grabbed a shuttle into the town of Julian about 30 minutes up a windy mountain road for a late lunch.

Stopped at the Julian Beer Company for BBQ and a beer (or two). Delicious!! 🤤 

Chatted with Steven a bit and hung out on the patio. 

Stopped at Ma's pie shop for a free slice of pie (complimentary to all PCT hikers). Packed it up to eat on trail.

Shuttled back to the bridge and started the climb up Grape one Mountain at dusk - beautiful sky.

Found a spot for two tents mostly near the top but with some shelter - setup tents and ate pie for dinner.

Time to get ready for bed - chores include but are not limited to: blowing up air mattress, clean feet/face/body, foot care/blister care (my feet are looking pretty sad and gross right now), change into comfy jammies/warmer clothes, reorganize pack, plan out food/snacks/meals for the next day, secure smelly items and food, charge up phone/watch/misc., take notes of day, read, go to sleep....

Camping here: GPS:[33.10931, -116.47876]

properties.name: Day 7 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #7: 04/11/2026 - Saturday 
Goal: halfway to Warner Springs 
Miles Hiked Today = 16.68 miles & 1.66 miles  (total of 18.34 miles today)
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 98
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 99.41
Miles to go = 1,002 miles // 1227

Up @ 5:30 AM - beautiful sunrise - pink puffy clouds everywhere! A morning bathroom hole with a view! 

On trail by 6:30 - ominous clouds catching on nearby mountain range - will it rain today or tonight?!

Trail is on the inner edge of the mountain range, weaving in and out along every inlet for miles and miles. The wind is BLOWING 🌬️ in strong nonstop gusts that almost blow you over - slamming into you.

...makes for some intense concentration while hiking on a mountain ledge...

Stopped for breakfast (Paul had leftovers from in town lunch yesterday - a Caesar salad and brisket sandwich!! I had cold soaked oatmeal 🫤).

Eventually made it to a wash and low point and out of the wind - stopped for some shade and snacks. Crazy jets doing low fly-by maneuvers in the valley - you hear them before you see them.

Also saw a chonky Horney Toad lizard - rusty mustard in color - so cute!

After a break we head up onto another mountain to continue the roundabout edge walking until we reach the water cache. 

Thankfully some friendly German hikers hauled up a bunch of water from the off trail cache (~ 1/2 mile or so one way) saving is some effort. Filled up and had lunch with another hiker - Aaron from Ohio.

Also found another NEW blister on my other heel - I don't feel them forming so can't be proactive! 🤬 I feel like my feet are entirely blistered now - two big blisters for feet. ... it's difficult to keep them clean when my feet are so dirty by the end of the day 😥

Goal to make it about 4 more miles to a wind buffered campsite - may prove tricky as so many hikers all bubbled together...

The camp spot was taken so we need to keep going at least another mile and a half. Passed many small single tent sites and a creepy little cave.

Paul almost fell off the mountain 😶 His pack got caught on a tree branch and he side stepped and fell off trail (on the side of a steep mountain) into a manzanita bush - Devon had to pull him back up. Thankfully no injuries - no death - only a rip in the pants. 

Finally found a spot and got setup but the wind was relentless and sooo strong and gusty - decided to pack up and find another spot.

Next campsite is about another mile and a half up trail... 😔

Got to the listed campsite - a wash - and there are several hikers already setup here - not much choice but to squeeze in or hike another 3+ miles.

I am setup near a man named Trout - he is hiking with fishing gear & he is also cowboy camping.

Setup tent - found a tick on my tent 🤮 - Trout was impressed I even saw the tick - He killed it! 

Ate a "Thanksgiving Dinner" in my tent.

It's getting chilly so I'm bundled up - I may have nightmares of wood ticks crawling all over me. 

Some sprinkles of rain at 2:30 or so in the morning.

Camping here: GPS:[33.19766, -116.57319]

properties.name: Day 8 - Camp
properties.description:
ONE WEEK ON TRAIL 🤯 ____

Day #8:  04/12/2026 - Sunday
Goal: Warner Springs - beat the rain 🌧️ 🌧️ 🌧️ 
Miles Hiked Today = 11.66 miles 
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 109.5 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 111.07 miles
Miles to go = 990.5 // 1,215.5

Cloudy morning - big white fluffy clouds stuck on the mountains - covering the rims of the sky.

Hiked through shrub covered mountains. Seeing views of prairie fields in the distance.

****Hit 100 miles on trail******

Oak trees lead to a water cache/tank. Happy frogs are chirping in the tank and around.

Road cross to "private property" and up into hills overlooking fields. Down into beautiful yellow and purple fields of grass for miles with rolling hills.

Clouds look beautiful and ominous - hope to beat the rain to Warner Springs.

Eagle rock  is true to name - can see it from a distance and becomes more distinct the closer you hike. 🪨🦅 

More fields turn to manzanita brush mazes turn to creeks surrounded by oak trees - so beautiful.

Made it to Warner Springs Community center:
Cold outdoor showers (for free)
Bucket laundry 🪣 
Charging station 
Food for sale and microwave and toaster to heat - for lunch I had two mini frozen pizzas, icecream bar, coconut water and sprite. Plantain chip for snack and Mountain house beef and tomato pasta, fruit bar, bubble water for dinner.

Still raining, very cold out! Hanging out at the community center with hikers all day. 

The owner offered to make an exception and let us use the facility for the night - about 25 people opted to stay dry and are sleeping on the floor of the community center. Slumber party!!

Camping here: GPS:[33.27444, -116.64500]

properties.name: Day 9 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #9:  04/13/2026 - Monday 
Goal: Post office pickup & back on trail 
Miles Hiked Today = 14.69 miles 
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 122.7 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 125.76 miles
Miles to go = 977.3 // 1202.3

Slept surprisingly pretty good - warm and dry!

Up at 5:30 AM before community center opens packed and waiting for breakfast burritos - delivered about 7:30 AM. Ate and off for a walk to the post office for another resupply box pickup.

Walked on side cut trail and road walk. 
Pink polkadot box ready and full of food. 🩷 
Organized resupply and continued road walk to get back on trail.

Area is beautiful with big trees and grass and mountains and more...

Didn't get too far before a hazy drizzle started - not enough for a rain jacket yet...

Hiked through trees and crisscrossed over streams. Sun came out - stopped for a snack and a break.

Sky still looks scary - didn't make it far before the rain started again, off and on drizzle and full rain and even some hail - brrr! You can see your breath 🌬️

Hiking up up up in the rain - can see the yellow grass fields from the other day and Warner Springs far off in the distance. 

A pocket of sun found us so we stopped and ate lunch. Very cold - need to keep moving.

Hiked up and around and up and around some more. Jeep road and big chunky boulder mountain in the distance. Beautiful Green valley.

Getting cold(er) and tired - found a squishy spot in the midst of some manzanita bushes. Setup tents quick ate a fast (cold soaked) dinner. 

Bathroom break before bed showed clouds coming in fast and low over surrounding mountains - mesmerizing to watch.

It's VERY chilly - likely to be our coldest night so far on trail.  Attempted some foot care (feet are not looking good) and bundled up for book and bed by 7 PM. 

Finished my book: The Year of Magical Thinking - Joan Didion.

Camping here: GPS:[33.34653, -116.61377]

properties.name: Day 10 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #10:  04/14/2026 - Tuesday 
Goal: ~ 15 + miles
Miles Hiked Today = 16.85 miles 
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 138.8 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 142.61 miles
Miles to go = 961.2 // 1186.2

Sooo cold - frost on tent - everything is wet (sleeping bag, tent, ground...) 

On trail by 6:45 - cold delay - can't feel my hands.

Climbed up with great views of green mountains and clouds stuck on top and in between. Terrain changed to big boulders covering the hills.

Paul is listening to an audio book about world war 2 and is retelling the book to us day by day - it's a fun way to pass miles!

Hiked to water tanks at 'Mike's place' - air-dried the wet tent and filtered and filled up water for the day - right before climbing up up up. 

The climb was worth the view - can see the San Jacinto mountains (where we'll be hiking next to Idyllwild) - they're snow kissed and beautiful!

Zigged and zagged down and around the mountain, then up and down through washes and brush.

Second water is off trail down a steep hill - a natural spring is being collected in a tank and then piped to a fire hose spigot - filtered and filled up.

Last few miles of the day to find a great big wash for spaces out sandy camping.

Cooked dinner and cleaned up and in bed by 7:30 PM. Excited to start a new book tonight:  The Gate of the Feral Gods - Matt Dinniman.

Coyotes yipping in the distance serenading us to sleep. 💤 💕

Camping here: GPS:[33.47164, -116.58602]

properties.name: Day 11 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #11:  04/15/2026 - Wednesday 
Goal: Paradise Valley Burgers & 16+ miles
Miles Hiked Today = 18.12 miles 
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 155.9 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 160.73 miles
Miles to go = 944.1 // 1169.1

Up earlier today - 5 AM wakeup.

Terrain is changing again - shrubs are spacing out more - big brown vast mountain ranges in the distance. Still weaving in and out and up and down - sometimes difficult to tell where you'll end up in the vast terrain ahead. ... sometimes the trail is visible miles ahead (most often a climb up and out of a valley.

Stopped to air out our wet gear from last night (practically everything!!) - had a snack sat on the sunshine and enjoyed the view.

Met two men - a professor and videographer documenting wildlife and plants along the PCT - they were observing a raven's nest in a telephone pole when we saw them.

Hiked up and around and down some more 🙃

Found a nice sandy shady spot for lunch - ramen - it was delicious 🤤 

Climbed some more (I'm sensing a trend here in these mountains...). Terrain on one side sandy cliffs that collapse down in sheer drops. We keep going up and in and out - uncertain where & when the very near road (only 2 miles) will show itself. 

Down we go (finally) and reach a row of telephone poles that will take us to Paradise Valley Cafe & burgers!!!

Hiked another mile (off trail) to get to the PV Cafe - VERY hiker friendly! We sat inside and ordered food - I got: a burger sweet potato fries (and normal fries because the waitress made a mistake), a coke, and a beer. Yum! 🤤💕

We hung out and route planned for the final push today and the next two days UP to Idyllwild - water looks like it's going to be a HEAVY carry - 5 liters (11 pounds) (to avoid going far off trail and adding miles and additional elevation gains/loss). 

Refilled water at the Cafe - scoured the hiker box for goodies (instant coffee ☕) washed up and hitched a ride back to the trail to save us an extra mile of walking.

Hiked another 3+ miles up through pine trees, massive boulders and green. Saw a baby rattle snake on trail. 

Made it to our spot and got settled in just at dusk - two other campers here!

Coyotes yipping 

Camping here: GPS:[33.59563, -116.57119]

properties.name: Day 12 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #12:  04/16/2026 - Thursday 
Goal: 15 miles up up up - major elevation today!
Miles Hiked Today = 15.65 miles 
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 170.9 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 176.38 miles
Miles to go = 929.1 // 1154.1

Up at 5 AM - on trail by 6 AM 

Beautiful colors in the sky - pinks and purples.

Hiking up. Through more bouldery terrain. 

Expansive views all day as we climb higher and higher. Rolling dry brown hills. As we turn and make our way to a saddle/ridge a new valley can be seen. A beautiful lake. Indio & Palm Springs, windmills, etc.

All the while the San Jacinto mountains are in the distance - we hike closer with each step!

Had lunch under a beautiful big pine 🙃

Some hills have sun bleached remains of manzanita bushes poking through manzanita bushes - they look like bones. 

Up up up up up up up.

Remnants of snow on trail.

Had to climb over about 6 downed trees and under two.

Brush very overgrown on the trail - need to push through - feels like a birthing experience (again and again).

The higher we climb the stronger the wind gusts get - VERY intense!!! Can feel the wind push you - scary on some parts of the trail!

Water source is off trail and down - Paul offered to make the trek while Devon & I hunker down in a ravine to stay out of the wind - it's so intense!

Hiked about 2 more miles to next campsite. Jaw dropping epic views the entire way.

Rounded a bend to the campsite - setup - it will be difficult to avoid the wind - Devon & Paul had their tent completely setup and took it down and moved it to another more sheltered spot.

Ate and got ready for bed - it's going to be a cold windy night. I'm wearing all of my layers 🥶

Had a beautiful moth come into my vestibule last night - he spent the night and stayed warm.

Camping here: GPS:[33.72777, -116.63302]

properties.name: Day 13 - Airbnb
properties.description:
Day #13:  04/17/2026 - Friday 
Goal: Idyllwild or bust!!!
Miles Hiked Today = 12.98 miles 
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 179.4 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 189.36 miles
Miles to go = 920.6  // 1145.6

My moth stayed all night tucked into my vestibule. Made sure he was set aside in a nearby log safely.

Very cold and still super windy - packed up as quick as possible. On trail before 6:30 AM.

Sunrise over Palm Springs - very hazy sky.

Heading up to the saddle today - still windy but less aggressive than the other day - thankfully as the terrain is a bit sketchy ahead.

Skirting the edge of the mountain range we go up. Many burned and downed trees - I lost count of how many we had to climb over today - we even had to crawl under one!

The views are so amazing - surreal! Mind bendingly beautiful 😍

Sheer white rocky faces, chunky boulders that make up an entire mountain. A point you think you are heading towards - disappears only to reappear after numerous switchbacks and twists and climbs.

Some rock slides to navigate and precarious edges and scrambles. Happy to not be alone on this section!

Nearing the saddle the terrain has changed from rocky to big beautiful pine trees and remnants of snow. Filtered fresh mountain spring water and continued on to the Devil's slide - a side trail that will bring us down down down into Idyllwild.

Hitched a ride and met a cute puppy named Nevada. Dropped off bags and went to La Casita Mexican restaurant for early dinner 🤤 

Walked back to the Airbnb and organized a few things (clothes, shoes, food, etc.) - will do a full inventory tomorrow.

Shower was amazing - dirty feet are much improved.

Airbnb (booked by Steven in advance of our arrival) is super cute and cozy. Little two bedroom cabin with wood paneling and retro decor. Big orange/red velvet couch.

Devon made rice crispy bars and we watched This is 40 snuggled up with sleeping bags on the couch.

Tomorrow will be a Near-O day (sadly not a zero) as we need to walk in and out of town... But happy to relax and let my feet heal up.

Airbnb here: GPS [33.7603980, -116.6956550]

properties.name: Day 14 - Airbnb
properties.description:
Day #14:  04/18/2026 - Saturday 
Goal: Movie and food in Idyllwild 
Miles Hiked Today = ZERO 
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 179.4 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 189.36 miles
Miles to go = 920.6  // 1145.6

First ZERO day on trail.

Slept in - read in bed for a bit and talked with Steven.

Read outside on the patio - Devon made French toast and bacon with fruit and coffee for breakfast 🤤 

Relaxed at Airbnb for the morning.

Hitched a ride into town - picked up PCT bag tags at Ranger Station & walked around town.

Went to the movies and saw the only movie playing in town: Normal - action dark comedy film - it was exactly what you'd expect - we had the entire theater to ourselves with popcorn and soda and snacks. 

Met the town mayor and his security backup (two beautiful golden retriever dogs).

Walked around town some more and went to the grocery store to resupply for the next week.

Went to Idyllwild brewpub had a drink and split a cob salad. 

Hitched a ride back to Airbnb.

Movie night, watched: I Feel Pretty and ate frozen pizzas and monkey bread.

Airbnb here: GPS [33.7603980, -116.6956550]

properties.name: Day 15 - Airbnb
properties.description:
Day #15:  04/19/2026 - Sunday 
Goal: Relax 
Miles Hiked Today = ZERO  / walked 3.05 miles
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 179.4 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 192.41 miles
Miles to go = 920.6  // 1145.6

Slept in today and woke to the smell of bacon.

Devon made French toast and bacon with coffee.

Lazy morning - eventually made our way out of the cabin. Walked into Idyllwild and wandered around town. Went to pharmacy and finished resupply.

Went to the Idyllwild brewpub and had a few drinks and hung out. 

Late lunch at Mama's Egg House - got a burger and sweet potato fries. 

Hitched a ride with a sweet old man to our Airbnb. Relaxed and read for the rest of the afternoon. 

Packed up hiking great and prepped for tomorrow. 

Big salad and cherry pie for dinner. Are on the couch and watched Civil War.

Airbnb here: GPS [33.7603980, -116.6956550]

properties.name: Day 16 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #16:  04/20/2026 - Monday 
Goal: Get back on trail!
Miles Hiked Today = 14.53
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 190.4 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 203.89 miles
Miles to go = 909.6  // 1134.6

Egg burritos 🌯 for breakfast with coffee. 

Washed dishes, finished packing and taped up feet. Temporary tattoos in place - Ready for the trail!!!

Our driver Grumpy showed up with Virginia and a new hiking friend Mat in the car - we smooshed in for a ride to the Humber Trailhead.

2.5 miles up Devil's slide to get back on the PCT. Thankfully conversation was flowing and we made pretty good time. 

Continued up to the trail split for the San Jacinto summit - Mat is hiking the summit. We had lunch before we split up. 

Mat shared the story of how he came to have a man's ashes/remains in his backpack... (When looking for a place to stay in town for the night a woman approached him and said she had a room. He went home with her and her behavior became odd and erratic - she started saying how much he reminded her of David her dead husband... Long story short she gave him some of her dead husband's remains to spread - Saying he'll know what to do with them David will tell him...) So for the moment he's hiking with them on trail...

Continued hiking with Virginia today on trail. She was a welcome distraction as today was very difficult - lots of elevation gain hit highest point yet on trail of 9,000+ ft elevation.

Many stream and creek crossings right on trail. Crisp clean mountain water to filter and drink. One almost waterfall with water flowing and streaming through big boulders and through trees over the trail and down the mountain (San Jacinto River).

Beautiful pines and boulders the size of houses. Some remains of snow hidden in shady crevices.

Vistas all day of distant mountain ranges - green on the east and brown on the west side of our climb. Windmill's visible on the west side in the valley.

Got to camp pretty late in the day. Very tired. Setup ate dinner as the sky went from purple and pink to neon pink and dark. 

Finished setup and cleanup and snuggled in to bed. 

Super windy all night - very loud (not calming white noise).

Camping here: GPS:[33.83958, -116.73661]

properties.name: Day 17 - Hostel
properties.description:
Day #17:  04/21/2026 - Tuesday 
Goal: Down down down the San Jacinto mountains (6,000+ ft elevation descent)
Miles Hiked Today = 19.66
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 209.6 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 223.5 miles
Miles to go = 890.4  // 1115.4

Slept through my alarm - up and packed quickly.

On trail ba little after 6 AM - today we descend over 6,000+ ft. 

The valley unfolds below - you can see glimpses of the wind turbines and the freeway and Casio. along with the mountains and clouds below in the distance.

Beautiful water stop off trail at a flowing creek.

Continued on down - getting windy! In reviewing camping options for the night it appears that everything will be crazy windy and sandy and not great. Reached out to the Hiker Haven hostel off trail and reserved the last three spots available!

Continued down all day amazing to see the San Jacinto peak tower over us as we descend - it is snow peaked and massive!

The trail isn't smooth and flowy - slightly technical in spots - so it's a bit slow going.

*****MILE 200**********

Wind is getting gusty - almost blows you off your feet - can be scary if you're near a ledge...

Rattle snake on trail - he got very feisty and didn't want to move. Eventually moved up the side wall but still in a striking pose. Waited a bit for him to settle down and zoomed past him quickly.🐍 🐍 

Virginia caught up with us on trail and hiked down the neverending switch backs. We are stuck on repeat in a neverending downward switch back hike.

Finally made it to the bottom - water spigot to fill up and filter some water. Chatted with some other hikers that were trying to figure out where to stay for the night to avoid the wind... Wished them luck and headed out as nasty looking clouds are moving in fast!

The trail turned into a road walk for a few miles. Clouds still coming in fast - looks like the top of the San Jacinto Mtns are getting dumped on.

Made it off the road to find some trail magic - cooler filled with Gatorade bottles. Enjoyed a cold drink before heading back on trail for one last push  to get to our hostel.

Hiked away from the San Jacinto Mtns, under power lines, adjacent to wind turbines, across washed out dirt roads, in the sandy sandy wash next to the train tracks and freeway, in the wind and some sprinkles until we reached the I-10 underpass.

Hiked through the creepy underpass and upp the road to the hiker hostel.

Upon arrival we were told to take off our shoes and wash our hands. We brought our bags in and were shown around. The home has three cats (two are kept in the garage) - as a result Devon took my bunk bedroom (she's allergic) and I got stuck with a squat spot in the garage with 5 guys (including Paul) and the cats! 🙃

We took turns using the shower and cleaning up - laundry was available as were loaner clothes to wear while yours are in the wash. We all got a set of pajamas to wear.

Ate dinner, ramen and bean soup (made by the host) and hung out with other hikers (12 of us in total).

The host had a crazy kid (5th grade) running around the house while we were there too - he was pretty damn annoying.

Slept poorly on the hard stinky garage floor - but ate well met some cool people and got a shower and clean clothes too!

** Longest mileage to date: 19.66 miles 🫠

Hostel Stay here: [33.92623, -116.69367]

properties.name: Day 18 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #18:  04/22/2026 - Wednesday 
Goal: Hike to Mission Creek ~ 16+ miles
Miles Hiked Today = 17.15
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 226.1 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 240.65  miles
Miles to go = 873.9  // 1098.9

Slow and lazy morning - coffee and breakfast and hanging out with hikers before packing up and getting back on trail.

Desert scrub, wind turbines and sand as we approach the San Bernardino Mountains.

Started 'section C of the PCT'

Up up up the valley to a ridgeline - vast mountains await! The trail winds down and onto a field of yellow grass amidst steep drops and peaks.

Eventually the whitewater river preserve becomes visible in the distance - bleached white giant river rocks. We wind our way down to the preserve. Cross a sandy boulder field.

Met up with another hiker - kaleidoscope - hiked with her the rest of the day.

River Crossing with water flowing fast and up to your knees - I took the time to take my shoes off and cross in crocs - no wet shoes for me!

We filtered water at the river, I soaked my feet and dunked my shirt for some relief from the heat on our next 5 mile climb.

Another angry rattlesnake on trail 🐍 🐍 got him off into the bushes and made as wide of an arc as possible to pass.

Made it down to the mission Creek campground - setup, filtered water, ate dinner & chatted with kaleidoscope - and went to bed.

Lulled to sleep by the sound of rushing river water and frog calls.

Camping here: GPS:[34.04260, -116.65693]

properties.name: Day 19 - Camp
properties.description:
Day #19:   04/23/2026 - Thursday 
Goal: Hike Mission Creek (boulder strewn damaged section of trail)
Miles Hiked Today = 14.42
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 239.9  miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 255.07  miles
Miles to go = 860.1  // 1085.1

Alarm clock Paul woke me up this morning with a shake on my tent. Sunrise is peaking through the mountains - pink and orange!

Packed up and started our hike up mission Creek. 

***The trail is known to be washed out and damaged due to a storm/hurricane from 2023. Today will require some route finding skills, river crossing, scrambling and boulder hopping.***

Start the day with a river crossing - no way to do it without getting your feet wet - here we go!

We had trail for about a mile and then it is washed away. Stopped for breakfast before things get crazy!

Metup with Kaleidoscope, Mrs. Pooh & Virginia and hiked and scrambled with them all day today.

Some of the trail is marked with cairns, sometimes you can see footprints... Mostly you follow the river that runs throughout a massive bed of boulders and sand and downed logs. Every so often you need to cross the creek to get on the other side as it gets next to the riverbed walls.

Walking is slow as you must pay attention to every step you take.

To get back on trail we had to scramble up the steep 8 ft. Tall wall of the riverbed. Packa were handed up and poles and Paul was needed to assist everyone to the top.

Eventually the trail forks and we ascend up up up a ridge and valley to get to another riverbed crossing.

Late lunch stopped and took off we're shoes and socks and lasted everything in the sun. My feet are pruney.

3 more miles to our campsite. 

The trail is off the riverbed and climbing but has been washed out in several places - requiring creative and somewhat scary crossings. Very helpful to gave others around to help and be second eyes and hands when needed. 

Eventually came down to a valley with creek crossing surrounded by boulders and the charred remains of trees. 

Trail very unclear from here - required a failed attempt and reroute to scramble and climb our way out.

Once out more climbing and winding on trail to reach our campsite. 

Setup tent and gear, hot cooked ramen with chicken cashews olive oil seasoning and peanut butter for dinner. Filtered water for tomorrow - exhausted.

Tomorrow is Steven  & Dan Day!! 🥳

Camping here: GPS:[34.12537, -116.75799]

properties.name: Day 20 - Airbnb
properties.description:
Day #20:   04/24/2026 - Friday 
Goal: STEVEN 😘💕
Miles Hiked Today = 12.52
Total miles Hiked on the PCT = 252.1 miles
Total miles Hiked (on & off trail) = 267.59  miles
Miles to go = 847.9  // 1072.9

Flowing trail today - up and down and spectacular views. 

Snowy prayed San Bernardino mountains.
Overlook on the Mission Creek Wash & the San Jacinto mountains .

Took a very long lunch break to takes and kill some time before meeting up with Steven & Dan in Big Bear.

A parade of hikers passed by while we having hung out. Manny New faces - most hikers we've seen in such a short time - we must be hitting a bubble near Big Bear.

Looking ahead at the weather forecast it is going to rain & snow - we opted for an Airbnb instead of freezing or butts off camping the next two nights with Steven & Dan. Everyone will be happier! 

**** Hit mile # 250 *****

Hikered off trail to Hwy 38 - to get a hitch into town. Waited about 15 minutes and got a ride with a man and his two kids. We were squished!! The car was a hatchback - Paul rode on the trunk, Devon up front and I was in the backseat with the kids (they were adorable)!

Does at our Airbnb and waited for Steven and Dan to show up. Upon arrival we unpacked a few things from the truck and then made for our priority. 

Paul picked out a place called Wyatt's Grill & Saloon. An eclectic community center/VFW vibes with a bar, food, dancefloor and stage. 

We ordered food (pizza for Steven & chicken Parmesan for me) and drinks and watched the ballroom dancing lessons.

 Eventually a band showed up and the dancing changed to line dancing - it was very entertaining. 

We went to the grocery store next to pick up food and supplies for breakfast. Why back to the Airbnb and hung out for a while before bed.

Staying here: Airbnb 914 Robinhood Blvd, Big Bear, CA 92314
